volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: Amphitryon (Section 2.2.664b - Section 2.2.725b)

sections.label: Section 2.2.664b
sections.original: Qua gratia ?
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.665
sections.original: Quia domi daturus nemo est prandium advenientibus .
sections.translation: Because there ’ s no person at home to give us a breakfast on our arrival .

sections.label: Section 2.2.666
sections.original: Qui tibi nunc istuc in mentemst ?
sections.translation: How comes that now into your mind ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.666b
sections.original: Quia enim sero advenimus .
sections.translation: Why , because we have come too late .

sections.label: Section 2.2.667
sections.original: Qui ?
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.667b
sections.original: Quia Alcumenam ante aedis stare saturam intellego .
sections.translation: Because I see Alcmena standing before the house , with her stomach-full [*] already .

sections.label: Section 2.2.668
sections.original: Gravidam ego illanc hic reliqui quóm abeo .
sections.translation: I left her pregnant here when I went away .

sections.label: Section 2.2.668b
sections.original: Ei perii miser .
sections.translation: Alas , to my sorrow , I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 2.2.669
sections.original: Quid tibi est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.669b
sections.original: Ad aquam praebendam commodum adveni domum ,
sections.translation: I have come home just in good time to fetch the water [*]

sections.label: Section 2.2.670
sections.original: decumo post mense , ut rationem te putare intellego .
sections.translation: in the tenth month after that , according as I understand you to compute the reckoning .

sections.label: Section 2.2.671
sections.original: Bono animo es .
sections.translation: Be of good heart .

sections.label: Section 2.2.671b
sections.original: Scin quam bono animo sim ? si situlam cepero ,
sections.translation: Do you know of how good heart I am ? By my troth , do you never after this day entrust to me aught that is sacred , if I don ’ t draw up all the life of that well , if I do but make a beginning .

sections.label: Section 2.2.674
sections.original: Sequere hac me modo ; alium ego isti rei allegabo , ne time .
sections.translation: Do you only follow me this way . I ’ ll appoint another person for that business ; don ’ t you fear .

sections.label: Section 2.2.675
sections.original: Magis nunc me meum officium facere , si huic eam advorsum , arbitror .
sections.translation: I think that I shall now be doing my duty more , if I go to meet him .

sections.label: Section 2.2.676
sections.original: Amphitruo uxorem salutat laetus speratam suam ,
sections.translation: With joy , Amphitryon greets his longed for wife — her , whom of all women in Thebes her husband deems by far the most excellent , and whom so much the Theban citizens truthfully extol as virtuous . Have you fared well all along ? Do I arrive much wished for by you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.679b
sections.original: Haud vidi magis .
sections.translation: I never saw one more so ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.680
sections.original: exspectatum eum salutat magis haud quicquam quam canem .
sections.translation: for she greets her own husband not a bit more than a dog .

sections.label: Section 2.2.681
sections.original: Et quom te † gravidam et quom te pulchre plenam aspicio , gaudeo .
sections.translation: When I see you pregnant , and so gracefully burdened , I am delighted .

sections.label: Section 2.2.682
sections.original: Obsecro ecastor , quid tu me deridiculi gratia
sections.translation: Prithee , in the name of all that ’ s good , why , for the sake of mockery , do you thus salute and address me , as though you hadn ’ t lately seen me — as though now , for the first time , you were betaking yourself homeward here from the enemy ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.685
sections.original: atque me nunc proinde appellas quasi multo post videris ?
sections.translation: For now you are addressing me just as though you were seeing me after a long time .

sections.label: Section 2.2.686
sections.original: Immo equidem te nisi nunc hodie nusquam vidi gentium .
sections.translation: Why , really for my part , I have not seen you at all this day until now .

sections.label: Section 2.2.687
sections.original: Cur negas ?
sections.translation: Why do you deny it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.687b
sections.original: Quia vera didici dicere .
sections.translation: Because I have learned to speak the truth .

sections.label: Section 2.2.687c
sections.original: Haud aequom facit
sections.translation: He does not do right , who unlearns the same that he has learned . Are you making trial what feelings I possess ? But why are you returning hither so soon ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.690
sections.original: an te auspicium commoratum est an tempestas continet
sections.translation: Has an ill omen delayed you , or does the weather keep you back , you who have not gone away to your troops , as you were lately speaking of ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.692
sections.original: Dudum ? quam dudum istuc factum est ?
sections.translation: Lately ? How long since was this lately ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.692b
sections.original: Temptas . iam dudum , modo .
sections.translation: You are trying me ; but very lately , just now .

sections.label: Section 2.2.693
sections.original: Qui istuc potis est fieri , quaeso , ut dicis : iam dudum , modo ?
sections.translation: Prithee , how can that possibly be as you say ?— but very lately , just now .

sections.label: Section 2.2.694
sections.original: Quid enim censes ? te ut deludam contra lusorem meum ,
sections.translation: Why , what do you imagine ? That I , on the other hand , shall trifle with you who are playing with me ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.695
sections.original: qui nunc primum te advenisse dicas , modo qui hinc abieris .
sections.translation: in saying that you are now come for the first time , you who but just now went away from here ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.696
sections.original: Haec quidem deliramenta loquitur .
sections.translation: Surely she is talking deliriously .

sections.label: Section 2.2.696b
sections.original: Paulisper mane ,
sections.translation: Stop a little while , until she has slept out this one sleep .

sections.label: Section 2.2.697b
sections.original: Quaene vigilans somniat ?
sections.translation: Is she not dreaming with her eyes open ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.698
sections.original: Equidem ecastor vigilo , et vigilans id quod factum est fabulor .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , for my part I really am awake , and awake I am relating that which has happened ; for , but lately , before daybreak , I saw both him ( pointing at SOSIA ) and yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.699b
sections.original: Quo in loco ?
sections.translation: In what place ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.700
sections.original: Hic in aedibús ubi tu habitas .
sections.translation: Here , in the house where you yourself dwell .

sections.label: Section 2.2.700b
sections.original: Numquam factum est .
sections.translation: It never was the fact .

sections.label: Section 2.2.700c
sections.original: Non taces ?
sections.translation: Will you not hold your peace ? What if the vessel brought us here from the harbour in our sleep ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.702
sections.original: Etiam tu quoque adsentaris húic ?
sections.translation: Are you , too , going to back her as well ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.702b
sections.original: Quid vis fieri ?
sections.translation: What do you wish to be done ? Don ’ t you know , if you wish to oppose a raving Bacchanal , from a mad woman you ’ ll render her more mad — she ’ ll strike the oftener [*] ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.705
sections.original: si obsequare , una resolvas plaga .
sections.translation: if you humour her , after one blow you may overcome her ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.705b
sections.original: At pol qui certa res
sections.translation: But , by my troth , this thing is resolved upon , somehow to rate her who this day has been unwilling to greet me on my arrival home .

sections.label: Section 2.2.707b
sections.original: Inritabis crabrones .
sections.translation: You ’ ll only be irritating hornets .

sections.label: Section 2.2.707c
sections.original: Tace .
sections.translation: You hold your tongue . Alcmena , I wish to ask you one thing .

sections.label: Section 2.2.708b
sections.original: Quid vis roga .
sections.translation: Ask me anything you please .

sections.label: Section 2.2.709
sections.original: Num tibi aut stultitia accessit aut superat superbia ?
sections.translation: Is it frenzy that has come upon you , or does pride overcome you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.710
sections.original: Qui istuc in mentemst tibi ex me , mi vir , percontarier ?
sections.translation: How comes it into your mind , my husband , to ask me that ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.711
sections.original: Quia salutare advenientem me solebas antidhac ,
sections.translation: Because formerly you used to greet me on my arrival , and to address me in such manner as those women who are virtuous are wont their husbands . On my arrival home I ’ ve found that you have got rid of that custom .

sections.label: Section 2.2.714
sections.original: Ecastor equidem te certo heri advenientem ílico ,
sections.translation: By my faith , indeed , I assuredly did both greet you yesterday , upon your arrival ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.715
sections.original: et salutavi et valuissesne usque exquisivi simul ,
sections.translation: at that very instant , and at the same time I enquired if you had continued in health all along , my husband , and I took your hand and gave you a kiss .

sections.label: Section 2.2.717
sections.original: Tun heri hunc salutavisti ?
sections.translation: What , did you welcome him yesterday ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.717b
sections.original: Et te quoque etiam , Sosia .
sections.translation: And you too , as well , Sosia .

sections.label: Section 2.2.718
sections.original: Amphitruo , speravi ego istam tibi parituram filium ;
sections.translation: Amphitryon , I did hope that she was about to bring you forth a son ; but she isn ’ t gone with child .

sections.label: Section 2.2.719b
sections.original: Quid igitur ?
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.719c
sections.original: Insania .
sections.translation: With madness .

sections.label: Section 2.2.720
sections.original: Equidem sana sum et deos quaeso , ut salva pariam filium .
sections.translation: Really I am in my senses , and I pray the Gods that in safety I may bring forth a son ; but ( to SOSIA ) hap-ill shall you be having , if he does his duty : for those ominous words , omen-maker , you shall catch what befits you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.723
sections.original: Enim vero praegnati oportet et malum et malum dari ,
sections.translation: Why really an apple [*] ought to be given to the lady thus pregnant , that there may be something for her to gnaw if she should begin to faint .

sections.label: Section 2.2.725
sections.original: Tu me heri hic vidisti ?
sections.translation: Did you see me here yesterday ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.725b
sections.original: Ego , inquam , si vis decies dicere .
sections.translation: I did , I say , if you wish it to be ten times repeated .
